title: Ensure that the --make-iptables-util-chains argument is set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the --make-iptables-util-chains argument is set to true
input_row.rationale: Kubelets can automatically manage the required changes to iptables based on how you choose your networking options for the pods. It is recommended to let kubelets manage the changes to iptables. This ensures that the iptables configuration remains in sync with pods networking configuration. Manually configuring iptables with dynamic pod network configuration changes might hamper the communication between pods/containers and to the outside world. You might have iptables rules too restrictive or too open.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep kubelet Verify that if the --make-iptables-util-chains argument exists then it is set to true.
input_row.remediation: Edit the /etc/kubernetes/kubelet file on each node and remove the --make-iptables- util-chains argument from the KUBELET_ARGS parameter. Based on your system, restart the kubelet service. For example: systemctl restart kubelet.service Impact: Kubelet would manage the iptables on the system and keep it in sync. If you are using any other iptables management solution, then there might be some conflicts. Default Value: By default, --make-iptables-util-chains argument is set to true.   161 | P a g e References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet/
input_row.impact: Kubelet would manage the iptables on the system and keep it in sync. If you are using any other iptables management solution, then there might be some conflicts. Default Value: By default, --make-iptables-util-chains argument is set to true.   161 | P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires verification of the kubelet process startup arguments on each node, specifically the presence and value of the --make-iptables-util-chains flag.
- This flag is set at the process level (systemd unit, command line, or kubelet config file on the host) and is not exposed via the Kubernetes API, kubectl, or any in-cluster resource.
- The audit procedure explicitly instructs to run a process listing (ps -ef | grep kubelet) on each node, which requires host-level access.
- There is no Kubernetes API or kubectl command that can retrieve the kubelet process arguments or configuration from within the cluster.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each Kubernetes node in the cluster.
2. Run the following command to list the kubelet process and its arguments:
   ```
   ps -ef | grep kubelet
   ```
3. Examine the output for the presence of the --make-iptables-util-chains argument.
4. If the argument is present, verify that it is set to true (i.e., --make-iptables-util-chains=true).
5. If the argument is absent, the default behavior is true (compliant).
6. Repeat for all nodes in the cluster.
7. Confirm compliance if all kubelet processes either do not specify the flag (default true) or explicitly set it to true.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
